whatThisIs: Build the federal COBRA continuation-coverage timeline for a qualifying event. Sophie Well takes the qualifying-event type (involuntary termination, reduction in hours, divorce, death of the covered employee, dependent aging out, Medicare entitlement) and the event date, then maps out the key COBRA deadlines: the 14-day employer election-notice deadline, the 60-day participant election window, the 45-day initial premium deadline, and the maximum coverage length - 18 months for the standard cases, 29 months with a disability extension, and 36 months for divorce, death, or Medicare-entitlement events.
whenToUse: Use this when an HR administrator needs to confirm a beneficiary's exact election or premium-due date, when a former employee is trying to figure out how long their COBRA window will last under 29 USC 1162, or when a benefits navigator is deciding whether ACA Marketplace SEP is a better path than electing COBRA. COBRA deadlines are strict and the penalty for missing the 60-day election window is loss of coverage with no further appeal - so an explicit date-by-date timeline catches the cliff before a beneficiary falls off it.
